title: Aberdeen AAS Operational Playbooks (August 2001): Engaging Aberdeen Workshop, Briefing Workshop, Market Knowledge Methodology, and Activity Methodologies
author: Aberdeen Group / Peter S. Kastner (program owner)
subject_domain: analyst training; consulting methodology; sales process; supplier briefing protocol
abstract: Five internal Aberdeen Group playbooks dated August 2001, comprising the operational backbone for the post-bubble Advisory Access Service (AAS) launch driven by Peter Kastner. The set includes the Engaging Aberdeen Workshop (positioning, branding, lifecycle consulting, competitive landscape, solution-selling), the Briefing Workshop (pre-briefing due diligence, vendor/product profiles, NDA ground rules, lead-up agenda control, wrap-up pitch), the Market Knowledge Workshop methodology (competitive analysis, product SWOT, market overview, segmentation, current positioning), and two activity-methodology spreadsheets (Go-to-Market and Market Knowledge) enumerating step-by-step tasks, task times, analyst outputs, and required skill sets. Together these define how Aberdeen analysts operationalized the rebranded AAS workshop family in late 2001.
importance_rationale: The most operationally specific surviving artifacts of Aberdeen's Aug 2001 AAS rollout; codifies the workshop curriculum that supported the pricing model defined in batch 4.
relevance_rationale: Provides reusable, durable methodology templates (briefing protocol, SWOT-driven positioning analysis, lifecycle consulting model) directly applicable to modern analyst-relations and B2B technology consulting workflows.
prescience_rationale: The Lifecycle Consulting framing, the supplier-product-stage taxonomy, and the structured pre-briefing due-diligence workflow anticipated practices that became standard in the post-2010 analyst-relations industry; the 'research made personal' positioning anticipated the personalization arc in B2B services.
